title: Expert-Specialized Fine-Tuning for Mixture-of-Experts Models
preview: Proposes fine-tuning strategies that specialise individual experts in MoE models to improve task-specific performance without full retraining.
summary: Shows that selectively fine-tuning individual expert subnetworks in a pre-trained MoE model achieves better task specialisation than global LoRA or full fine-tuning at equivalent parameter budget. Introduces routing regularisation to maintain expert load balance during specialised training.
datacenter: Relevant to inference fleet operators maintaining multiple fine-tuned model variants. Expert-specialised fine-tuning could enable a single shared-weight base model with swappable expert adapters, reducing storage costs and enabling faster model updates.
metrics: Key result signal: task-specific accuracy improvement versus standard LoRA fine-tuning at equivalent compute budget.
